risks_fired.evidence: Body callout immediately before the prompt fence ('Claude figures out `<repo-name>` from the working directory. Help it if it gets confused.') names the resolution mechanism and gives the student the recovery move. Body-callout mitigation per judge template § 'Body-callout carve-out' applies — callout is directly adjacent, names the failure mode, gives student action. Downgraded from med (prior run) to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Mitigated by body callout. If further hardening wanted: add self-resolution instruction inside the fence ('Determine the repo name from `basename $(pwd)` and substitute in all paths below') to make it fully self-contained without body dependency.

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt chains four distinct sub-steps (find branch, create worktree, copy files, report) with no `No preamble` suppressor and no body primer after the fence naming preamble + skim-past cue. Rule 17 structural trigger fires on 4+ named sub-steps. Post-fence body callout addresses multi-branch disambiguation, not preamble behavior.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add a body primer immediately after the closing fence: 'Claude will narrate before acting; look for the shell command output confirming the worktree path and which files copied across.' No prompt edit required.

risks_fired.evidence: In-fence structural enforcement (`no narrative, no hedging`, binary shipped/not-shipped list, explicit no-qualification language) plus body callout naming RLHF softening risk neutralise. §50 convenience-bias carve-out applies.

`verifier — last output line of each run, pasted verbatim` forces literal artifact evidence. Binary list with no-qualification language prevents inflation. Body callout names risk and gives push-back move. §50 carve-out applies.
